title: Accessing Shared Network Drives and File Shares
text:
Q: How do I access shared network drives and file shares?
A: To access shared network drives and file shares, follow the steps for your operating system below. 

Windows:
1. Open 'File Explorer' by clicking on the folder icon in the taskbar or pressing 'Windows + E'.
2. In the left sidebar, click on 'This PC'.
3. At the top menu, click on 'Computer' and then select 'Map network drive'.
4. Choose a drive letter from the dropdown menu.
5. In the 'Folder' field, enter the network path (e.g., \\servername\sharename) or click 'Browse' to locate it.
6. If you want the drive to reconnect at login, check the box for 'Reconnect at sign-in'.
7. Click 'Finish'.
8. If prompted, enter your network credentials (username and password) and click 'OK'.

macOS:
1. Open 'Finder' from the Dock.
2. In the menu bar at the top of the screen, click on 'Go' and then select 'Connect to Server'.
3. In the 'Server Address' field, enter the network path (e.g., smb://servername/sharename) and click 'Connect'.
4. If prompted, enter your network credentials (username and password) and click 'Connect'.
5. Choose the shared folder you wish to access and click 'OK'.
6. The shared drive will appear on your desktop and in the Finder sidebar for easy access.
